body_txt: Some animals are named after what they are . Some animals are named after the food that comes from them . And some are named after the food they eat themselves. Naming an animal after their Stock Animal Diet provides a memorable name while playing on a trait most people know about. If the animal is a Funny Animal , it's most likely their surname, but could also be the first name; if they're a normal or Nearly Normal Animal named by humans they probably have Only One Name . Mice and rats named after cheese are particularly common, probably because there are so many different kinds of cheese. Carnivores are tricky, unless they're given Luke Nounverber names (e.g. a lion named Zebrahunter, rather than just Zebra), otherwise they look like A Dog Named "Cat" . Naming dogs after bones or meat byproducts works, though, and so does naming cats after fish. Compare and contrast A Pig Named "Porkchop" , for when an animal is named after a product that comes from the animal themselves. Subtrope of Edible Theme Naming .
